title: [non-record track] BankLinear: cross-layer shared weight bank with learned + random mixtures
body:
## Summary

This PR introduces **BankLinear**, where per-layer weights are constructed as mixtures over a shared bank of learned and fixed random matrices reused across layers.

This replaces explicit per-layer weight storage with **compositional weight synthesis**, enabling parameter reuse across depth while maintaining per-layer specialization.

---

## Setup

- Fixed 10k training steps (no wallclock constraint)
- Same architecture and training setup as the naive baseline
- BankLinear applied to QKV projections
- Saved parameters reinvested into larger MLP (2.65× vs 2.00×)

---

## Results

| Model | Seed | Pre-quant BPB ↓ | Post-quant BPB ↓ | Size (bytes) |
|-------|------|----------------:|-----------------:|-------------:|
| Baseline | 1337 | 1.2262 | 1.2328 | 15861272 |
| BankLinear | 1337 | **1.2245** | **1.2311** | 15729669 |
| Baseline | 42 | 1.2276 | 1.2343 | 15856563 |
| BankLinear | 42 | **1.2239** | **1.2300** | 15734621 |
| Baseline | 2025 | 1.2253 | 1.2321 | 15853892 |
| BankLinear | 2025 | **1.2223** | **1.2280** | 15739602 |
| **Average (Baseline)** | — | 1.2264 | 1.2331 | 15857242 |
| **Average (BankLinear)** | — | **1.2236** | **1.2297** | 15734631 |


---

## Throughput

BankLinear introduces additional overhead from weight composition and MLP expansion, resulting in ~1.25× slower training compared to the baseline in the current implementation.

As a result, it is not currently competitive on the time-constrained leaderboard, but may benefit from further optimization.



See README for further details and discussion.
